---

# Core Business Rules — Sistema de Reservas

## System Identity

- **Role**: Backend administrative panel for businesses (tenants)
- **NOT** the client-facing booking UI — this is the operator/admin backend
- **Region**: Bolivia — timezone `America/La_Paz`
- **Phone format**: `+591 XXXXXXXX` (Bolivian numbers)
- **Currency**: Bolivianos (Bs), stored as `integer × 100` (centavos)

## Multi-Tenancy (Business / Tenant Isolation)

Every piece of data belongs to a Business. ALL queries on tenant-scoped models must include `business_id` or reach it through a relationship.

```
Business (tenant)
├── categories
│   └── items (Reservable or MenuItem)
│       ├── pricing_rules
│       └── reservations
│           ├── reservation_items
│           ├── waitlists
│           └── reviews
├── promotions
└── notifications
```

**Scoping rule**: A controller serving a business owner must always scope to `auth()->user()->business_id` or verify ownership before returning data.

```php
// Always scope, never trust user input for business_id filtering
$items = Item::forBusiness($authenticatedBusinessId)->active()->get();
```

## Timezone

All date/time output to clients must be in `America/La_Paz` (UTC-4, no DST).

```php
// config/app.php
'timezone' => 'America/La_Paz',

// When creating Carbon instances
Carbon::now('America/La_Paz')
Carbon::parse($scheduledDate, 'America/La_Paz')
```

Store dates in the database in UTC. Display in Santa Cruz time.

## Business Statuses and Behavior

| Status | Can Accept Reservations? | Visible in Catalog? |
|---|---|---|
| `active` | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |
| `inactive` | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| `suspended` | ❌ No | ❌ No |

Validate `business.status === 'active'` before creating any reservation.

## Item Types

| Type | `is_reservable` | `is_menu_item` | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `reservable` | true | false | Mesa, cancha, espacio privado |
| `menu_item` | false | true | Plato, bebida, combo |

A `reservation` requires exactly one `reservable` item. It can have zero or more `menu_item` items as pre-order (stored in `reservation_items`).

## Capacity and Party Size

- `items.capacity` = maximum people that fit in the reservable space
- `reservations.party_size` must be `<= items.capacity`
- Validation must happen in `ReservationService`, not just FormRequest

```php
if ($request->party_size > $item->capacity) {
    throw new CapacityExceededException(
        "Party size {$request->party_size} exceeds capacity {$item->capacity}"
    );
}
```

## Anti Double-Booking

Two-layer protection:
1. **DB layer**: unique index on `[item_id, scheduled_date, start_time]`
2. **Code layer**: overlap check with `lockForUpdate()` in a transaction (see `laravel-service-action` skill)

The DB index catches exact-start collisions. The code-layer overlap check catches any slot that overlaps in duration.

## Confirmation Code

Every `Reservation` gets a unique `confirmation_code` on creation (generated by `ReservationObserver`). This code is shown to the client and scanned by the business at point of service.

Format: 8 uppercase alphanumeric characters, e.g. `A3K9ZQ7M`.

## Notification Events (when they fire)

| Event | Notified Party | Channels |
|---|---|---|
| Reservation created | Customer | WhatsApp, database |
| Reservation confirmed | Customer | WhatsApp, database |
| Reservation cancelled | Customer | WhatsApp, database |
| Reminder 2h before | Customer | WhatsApp |
| Waitlist slot available | Waiting user | WhatsApp, database |
| New review reply | Reviewer | database |

All notifications use Laravel's `Notifiable` trait. WhatsApp uses a custom HTTP driver.

## Reservation Source Analytics

Always capture `source` on every reservation. This is used for conversion analytics.

| Source | When |
|---|---|
| `app` | Created via mobile app |
| `web` | Created via web frontend |
| `walk_in` | Created by staff for walk-in customer |
| `phone` | Created by staff based on phone call |

Default: `app`. Never leave `source` null.

## Data Integrity Rules

- A `review` can only be left for a `reservation` with `status = completed`
- A user can only leave **one review per reservation**
- `reservation_items` rows cascade-delete when the reservation is deleted
- `items` with active reservations (`pending` or `confirmed`) cannot be deleted (`restrictOnDelete`)
- Promotions with past `ends_at` are immutable — do not modify, create a new one
- Price snapshots are **write-once**: set on reservation creation, never update

## User Model Rules

- `phone` is the unique identifier (unique index)
- `email` is optional (nullable)
- Users are created silently via `firstOrCreate` in `OtpAuthService` ("shadow accounts")
- No password field — authentication is 100% OTP
- Sanctum tokens have configurable expiration (default 30 days)
- Each device login generates an independent token with a `device_name`
